lessons.title: Python Introduction
lessons.content:
What is Python?

  • Python is a popular programming language. It was created by Guido van Rossum, and released in 1991.
  • It is used for: web development (server-side), software development, mathematics, system scripting.

What can Python do?

  • Python can be used on a server to create web applications.
  • Python can be used alongside software to create workflows.
  • Python can connect to database systems. It can also read and modify files.
  • Python can be used to handle big data and perform complex mathematics.

lessons.title: Python Get Started
lessons.content:
Python Install

Many PCs and Macs will have python already installed.

To check if you have python installed on a Windows PC, search in the start bar for Python or run the following on the Command Line (cmd.exe):

python --version

Python Quickstart

Python is an interpreted programming language, this means that as a developer you write Python (.py) files in a text editor and then put those files into the python interpreter to be executed.

lessons.title: Python Syntax
lessons.content:
Execute Python Syntax

As we learned in the previous page, Python syntax can be executed by writing directly in the Command Line:

>>> print("Hello, World!")

Hello, World!

Python Indentation

Indentation refers to the spaces at the beginning of a code line.

Where in other programming languages the indentation in code is for readability only, the indentation in Python is very important.

Python uses indentation to indicate a block of code.

lessons.title: Python Comments
lessons.content:
Creating a Comment

Comments starts with a #, and Python will ignore them:

# This is a comment
print("Hello, World!")

lessons.title: Python Variables
lessons.content:
Creating Variables

Variables are containers for storing data values.

Python has no command for declaring a variable.

A variable is created the moment you first assign a value to it.

x = 5
y = "John"
print(x)
print(y)

lessons.title: Python Data Types
lessons.content:
Built-in Data Types

In programming, data type is an important concept.

Variables can store data of different types, and different types can do different things.

  • Text Type: str
  • Numeric Types: int, float, complex
  • Sequence Types: list, tuple, range
  • Mapping Type: dict
  • Set Types: set, frozenset
  • Boolean Type: bool

lessons.title: Python Numbers
lessons.content:
Python Numbers

There are three numeric types in Python:

  • int
  • float
  • complex
x = 1 # int
y = 2.8 # float
z = 1j # complex

lessons.title: Python Casting
lessons.content:
Specify a Variable Type

There may be times when you want to specify a type on to a variable. This can be done with casting.

  • int() - constructs an integer number from an integer literal, a float literal (by removing all decimals), or a string literal (providing the string represents a whole number)
  • float() - constructs a float number from an integer literal, a float literal or a string literal (providing the string represents a float or an integer)
  • str() - constructs a string from a wide variety of data types, including strings, integer literals and float literals

lessons.title: Python Strings
lessons.content:
Strings

Strings in python are surrounded by either single quotation marks, or double quotation marks.

'hello' is the same as "hello".

print("Hello")
print('Hello')

lessons.title: Python Booleans
lessons.content:
Boolean Values

In programming you often need to know if an expression is True or False.

You can evaluate any expression in Python, and get one of two answers, True or False.

When you compare two values, the expression is evaluated and Python returns the Boolean answer:

print(10 > 9) # True
print(10 == 9) # False
print(10 < 9) # False

lessons.title: Python Operators
lessons.content:
Python Operators

Operators are used to perform operations on variables and values.

  • Arithmetic operators
  • Assignment operators
  • Comparison operators
  • Logical operators
  • Identity operators
  • Membership operators
  • Bitwise operators

Which data type is immutable in Python?
How do you start a comment in Python?
What is the result of 2 ** 3 in Python?
Which operator is used for floor division?
